commit.message:
Add full finance toolkit — 7 tools in one app

- app.py: home page with tool overview
- pages/1: 5-Year Financial Model (moved from root)
- pages/2: Annual Budget (existing)
- pages/3: Lending Calculator (loan + mortgage with early repayment)
- pages/4: Portfolio Analysis (Sharpe, VaR, risk/return scatter)
- pages/5: Stock Comparison (2 stocks, correlation, ratios)
- pages/6: Commodities Dashboard (gold, silver, oil, gas, copper, wheat)
- pages/7: Stock Analysis (any ticker, MAs, volatility, company info)

All charts rebuilt in Plotly. Consistent styling across all pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Annual Budget tool (1-year P&L with monthly breakdown)

- Sales forecast, direct costs, overheads by category
- Monthly P&L table with FY totals and % of revenue
- KPI cards: Revenue, Gross Profit, Overheads, EBITDA
- 4 charts: Revenue vs Costs, Monthly EBITDA, Overhead breakdown, Cumulative EBITDA
- Excel template download + upload
- PDF export with branded report

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: 5-year financial model

These path context notes were remnants from the previous project
and are not applicable to the current inertia-manipulation project.

style: redesign navbar icons and mobile tab-bar layout

Redraw all four SVGs for clarity at small sizes: hardcover
cookbook with curved pages and visible binding, Thai mortar
and pestle, sharp-cornered restaurant menu card, classic
shopping cart. Bump icon size from 1rem to 1.15rem with
heavier 1.8 stroke weight. Mobile view switches from
hidden labels to a tab-bar layout with icons above tiny
labels, matching familiar mobile app patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move silhouette/glow to outer container, anchor tower to bottom

- SVG silhouette and radial glow now position relative to full
  container (centered on screen) instead of the rows div
- justify-end pb-8: tower grows upward from the bottom
- Dynamic --rise-dist per row for foundation-up animation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Anchor tower to bottom so words build up from foundation

justify-end pb-8: single word sits at screen bottom, subsequent
words stack upward — visually builds from the ground up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dynamic rise distance: each row rises from proportional depth below base

Bottom row rises 40px, top row rises (N-1)*50+40px — words physically
travel from the foundation level up to their final position.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase towerRowIn translateY for more dramatic rise from base

60px travel distance makes each row visibly rise up from below
instead of barely-noticeable 20px nudge.

docs: update CLAUDE.md with 2026-03-05 session — QA fixes, tech debt

- Date updated to 2026-03-05
- App.jsx line count: ~2600 → ~3,280
- Frontend architecture: document lazy-mount + display:none tab pattern
- VALID_PAGES: add 'citations'
- Deployment: add Dockerfile non-root user warning (root required for Railway volumes)
- Change log: add 8884c6b (Dockerfile fix) and 531fe4e (QA fixes) entries
- Bug fix log: document all 6 QA fixes from 531fe4e
- Pending TODOs: add deploy checklist item
- New section: Known Technical Debt (SQLite, monolithic App.jsx, Starter 1-site limit, Citation Tracker data quality, no test suite)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

OCPBUGS-77054: Fix Add page issues with PF 6.4.0 and masonry layout (#16045)

This fixes multiple issues on the Add page:

1. Console accessibility errors for Card components (PF 6.4.0)
2. Add page cards not appearing on first load
3. Add page cards not clickable (PF 6.4.0)
4. Infinite re-rendering when browser DevTools is open

PatternFly 6.4.0 compatibility changes:
- Remove unnecessary isClickable/isSelectable props from GettingStartedExpandableGrid
- Add isControlled={false} to SimpleList (PF 6.4.0 changed default to true)
- Replace navigateTo with useNavigate hook and add e.preventDefault()

Masonry layout fixes:
- Move MeasuredItem component outside render function to prevent re-creation
- Add requestAnimationFrame for initial measurement timing
- Add debouncing and proper cleanup for resize handling
- Wrap components in memo for performance optimization

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(cda): introduce subsumption bundle and pushback validation mechanics (#19000)

Implements the Constructive Dissent Assurance (CDA) module as defined
in the pushback-over-validation subsumption plan. This operationalizes
the concept that healthy pushback > passive validation.

Includes:
- structured dissent schema
- decision review pipeline stage
- validation bias score metrics
- documentation and runbooks
- monitoring script
- subsumption bundle manifest and fixtures

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task
[5407182300386737700](https://jules.google.com/task/5407182300386737700)
started by @BrianCLong*

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Introduced automated weekly drift monitoring for validation bias
metrics
* Launched "Pushback Over Validation" governance capability with dissent
procedures, override requirements (2 maintainers), and incident playbook

* **Documentation**
* Added comprehensive governance standards, runbooks, and data-handling
guidelines for the new capability
* Defined SLO: 95%+ decisions include dissent; alert threshold at bias
score > 0.6
  * Updated repository assumptions documentation

* **Chores**
  * Added monitoring workflow automation and governance manifests

v22.1: Phase D Intelligence Layer — 4 AI enhancements

D1: Vendor freshness tracking with enrichedAt timestamps, stale detection (7-day TTL), refresh button on vendor detail cards, and freshness badges
D2: AI narrative for sensitivity analysis — generates buyer-specific decision insight explaining what weight sensitivity means for THIS buyer
D3: AI vendor-specific negotiation coaching — per-vendor coaching with opening position, pricing levers, competitive leverage, and contract red flags using actual evaluation data
D4: AI velocity analysis in decision fatigue panel — evaluation pace rating, bottleneck detection, estimated days to decision, and proactive next-step recommendations

v3.1: Add Polymath co-pilot — the 14th skill that thinks with you

New: Polymath skill — the user's co-pilot for exploration, research,
codebase onboarding, and decision-making. Features 6 modes (onboard,
research, ideate, advise, translate, synthesize), web search as primary
research tool, and structured options-first interaction model.

Integration:

commit.message:
feat(governance): enforce babel street trust principles in schema (#18999)

This pull request operationalizes the principles from the Babel Street
article "Designing Trustworthy AI for OSINT" by implementing strict
schema enforcements in the CI verification gates.

It accomplishes the following:
* **Removes Schema Bypass**: Eliminates the excessively permissive
"additionalProperties: true" schema rule that allowed simple reports to
bypass verification of the required trust gate fields.
* **Traceability and Explainability Enforcement**: Mandates new fields
(`source_nodes`, `contradiction_nodes`, `reasoning_steps`,
`confidence_level`, `claim_ref`) for evidence records matching the
trusted path patterns to guarantee output determinism and auditable
provenance.
* **Subsumption Master Plan Scaffold**: Implements a verified Master
Plan inside `subsumption/babel-street-trust` asserting how Babel
Street's themes map into Summit architecture patterns (e.g. Graph-First
evidence ledger, Human-in-the-Loop configurations).
* **Strategic Security Doctrinal Memo**: Adds
`docs/security/trustworthy-ai-osint.md` targeting the specific
architectural integration of "Trust as Code".

**Tests Performed:**
* Schema validation verified against existing and new test report
formats via `python3 evidence/validate.py`.
* CI Bundle verified via `node scripts/ci/verify_subsumption_bundle.mjs
subsumption/babel-street-trust/manifest.yaml`.
* Global links checks evaluated and confirmed with `npx tsx
scripts/check-doc-links.ts`.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task
[16795980244870180629](https://jules.google.com/task/16795980244870180629)
started by @BrianCLong*

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Documentation**
* Added comprehensive trustworthy-AI guidance for OSINT, codifying five
core trust criteria and practical implementation directives.

* **New Features**
* Evidence/reporting now includes standardized evidence IDs, source
lineage, confidence/uncertainty flags, and structured reasoning to
improve explainability and replayability.
* Introduced a governance manifest and trust claims to enable
policy-driven controls and human-in-the-loop adjudication.

* **Chores**
* Added CI verification gates, reproducible audit artifacts/metrics, and
test fixtures; updated release metadata and package versions.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: allow self-signed SSL for Railway Postgres

Railway uses self-signed certificates, causing SELF_SIGNED_CERT_IN_CHAIN
errors with rejectUnauthorized: true. Detect Railway URLs and disable
strict SSL validation for them while preserving it for other providers.

commit.message:
fix(mobile-notes): render overlay via createPortal to escape body overflow clipping

iOS Safari treats overflow-x: hidden on body as a containing block for fixed
elements, clipping the right edge of the fullscreen notes modal. Rendering
directly into document.body via createPortal bypasses all ancestor overflow
constraints and guarantees true full-viewport coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(transport): TimeoutStream registers independent wakeup for timeout

TimeoutStream::new previously used Sleep::with_time_getter which skips
timer driver registration and fallback thread spawning. This meant the
timeout could only fire when the inner stream was polled for other
reasons — if a sender goes silent, the timeout never fires and resources
leak. Now uses Sleep::new for wall-clock case and polls Sleep as a
Future so it registers with the timer driver or spawns a fallback thread.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: vsrx-parity — IPIP tunnels, qualified-next-hop, rib inet6.0, per-unit tunnels

Add IPIP tunnel support (ip-X/X/X interfaces) alongside existing GRE,
with routing-instance destination for VRF-scoped outer packets. Extend
tunnel schema with source, destination, mode, key, ttl, keepalive
fields. Per-unit tunnel configs now create separate Linux interfaces
(base name for unit 0, baseNameuN for unit N>0).

Add qualified-next-hop with interface for link-local IPv6 next-hops,
compiled to FRR static routes with interface specification. Support
rib <instance>.inet6.0 syntax for per-VRF IPv6 static routes in both
global routing-options and routing-instances.

Propagate inet6 static routes from routing-instances to FRR config.
Fix unreachable code in CLI. 16 new tests, 1050 total passing.
